step.title: Tier-1 Codebase Orientation
step.goal: Build a high-level mental map of the codebase shape — just enough context to write informed subagent prompts in ticket-grill. Tier 1 means directory tree, top-level configs, and a handful of representative source files. Anything deeper goes through a subagent during ticket-grill.

substep.title: Repo Top-Level Survey
substep.action: List the repository root. Identify: - The runtime/language signals (package.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, go.mod, etc.) - The build/test tooling (Makefile, justfile, CI configs) - The source root directory convention (src/, app/, cmd/, lib/) - The presence of docs/, thoughts/, specs/, or other documentation regions

substep.title: Read Top-Level Configs
substep.action:
Read the project's top-level config files for runtime and dependency signals. Examples: - package.json -> dependencies, scripts, runtime version - pyproject.toml -> Python version, packages, build system - tsconfig.json -> TypeScript compilation strictness - Cargo.toml -> Rust dependencies and features - README.md -> the project's stated purpose
Capture the minimal set: language(s), framework(s) in use, primary runtime, test framework. Do not deep-dive — note and move on.

substep.title: Sample Representative Source Files
substep.action:
Read 2-4 source files that represent the codebase's idioms. Pick files that show: - The dominant module layout (one example) - The dominant request/response or function-shape pattern (one example) - The dominant test pattern (one example) - Any cross-cutting infrastructure entry point (if obvious — e.g., src/index.ts, src/main.py)
These reads are for IDIOM RECOGNITION only. Do not start analyzing the request against these files yet — that's ticket-grill's job via subagent dispatch.

substep.title: Display Orient Summary

section.title: Tier 1 vs Tier 2 boundary
section.description: Tier 1 = orient. Tier 2 = research. The boundary is operational: - Tier 1 allowed in ticket-orient: ls, glob, top-level config reads, README, a handful of representative source files. - Tier 2 forbidden in ticket-orient: "find all usages of X", "analyze how feature Y works", "trace the data flow through module Z" — these go through codebase-locator / codebase-analyzer / pattern-finder subagents during ticket-grill.

rules.rule: If a substep is tempted to read more than ~5 files, it's drifting into Tier 2 — stop and let ticket-grill handle it
If a substep requires understanding HOW something works (not WHAT exists), it's Tier 2 — defer
Bundle files and thoughts/ artifacts are exempt: unrestricted direct reads per spec §7.2

section.title: Why orient, why not skip
section.description: Per QRDSPIV's "orient before questioning" principle, an agent cannot formulate informed questions without basic context. Skipping tier-1 leads to subagent prompts like "is there a database in this codebase?" when ls would have shown a prisma/ directory. The cost is small (a few file reads); the value is question quality.

rules.rule: Stop when you can articulate the codebase's shape in 5-7 lines (language, runtime, framework, source layout, test approach, documentation regions, any obvious special concerns)
Stop if you find yourself wanting to "just check one more file" — that's Tier 2 drift
Stop and proceed even if uncertain about idioms; ticket-grill's subagents will clarify

example.name: TypeScript backend
example.output: - Language: TypeScript - Runtime: Node.js (20.x from package.json engines) - Framework: Express, Sequelize ORM - Test framework: Jest - Source layout: src/{models,routes,middleware,utils} - Documentation regions: docs/adr/, thoughts/shared/ Files sampled: src/index.ts, src/models/user.ts, src/routes/tasks.ts, src/middleware/auth.ts

example.name: Python CLI
example.output: - Language: Python (3.11+) - Runtime: uv-managed venv - Framework: Typer (CLI), Pydantic (validation) - Test framework: pytest - Source layout: src/{package}/{cli,core,models} - Documentation regions: none-detected Files sampled: src/myapp/cli.py, src/myapp/core/engine.py, tests/test_cli.py
